perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" means all non-public information of CMHC and the Absence Management Services, and all personal information of CMHC employees, disclosed to the Proponent in aggregate or otherwise, including, without limitation, any functional, technical, operational and business information re lating to CMHC or its Absence Management Program including, but not limited to, demographics, policies, procedures, absence management details, financial data, product/service specifications and designs, data models, member data, medical data, return to work plans and communication plans. It also includes information orally disclosed if the disclosing party indicates at the time of disclosure the confidential or proprietary nature of the information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" means non-public information of CMHC disclosed to the Proponent. It also includes information orally disclosed if the disclosing party indicates at the time of disclosure the confidential or proprietary nature of the information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific details of what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' particularly the reference to 'Absence Management Services' and 'personal information of CMHC employees,' the modified definition becomes vague. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.1, where it is stated that confidentiality obligations are set out in 'this agreement'. But the specifics, such as 'policies, procedures, absence management details, financial data' have been removed from the definition of 'Confidential Information' clause, which generates uncertainty on how to enforce the contract, because it's now not clear what information the proponent is agreeing to protect in section 2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Confidentiality. The Proponent shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence, and shall not disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information to any person or entity outside of the Proponent without the prior written consent of CMHC. The Proponent shall employ such precautions as are necessary to prevent unauthorized use, access to and disclosure of Confidential Information, including but not limited to, ensuring that Confidential Information is disclosed only to those of its and its affiliates’ officers and employees who: (a) have a need to know the same; (b) have been advised of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, and the confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement; and (c) who are bound by a similar duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Confidential Information' omits specific examples, such as 'Absence Management Services' and 'personal information of CMHC employees,' which were present in the original text. This omission creates a contradiction with Section 2.1, which states that confidentiality obligations are set out in 'this agreement'. By removing specifics from the definition in clause 1.1, it becomes unclear what the Proponent is agreeing to protect in Section 2.1, leading to a strong contradiction regarding the scope and enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 Use. The Proponent agrees to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of making decisions related to the preparation and submission of a proposal in response to the RFP and not for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Use. The Proponent agrees to use the Confidential Information for the purpose of making decisions related to the preparation and submission of a proposal in response to the RFP .
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'solely' and 'not for any other purpose', this section introduces ambiguity. The Proponent can claim they used the information for preparing the RFP, while simultaneously using it for another undeclared purpose. Section 3.1's claim that a breach would result in 'serious and irreparable harm' is now questionable as the scope of permissible use is less clear and creates an in-text contradiction, by creating uncertainty of how the contract can be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Proponent agrees that a breach by it of any term or condition of this Agreement would result in serious and irreparable harm to CMHC which could not be completely compensated by monetary damages. The Proponent expressly agrees that CMHC shall be entitled to secure an appropriate legal remedy, including injunction or declaratory judgment, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any term of this Agreement to enable CMHC to protect its rights hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'solely' and 'not for any other purpose' from Section 2.2 weakens the restriction on how the Confidential Information can be used. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which assumes a strict interpretation of the use restriction, creating a situation where a breach is harder to define and prove, thereby contradicting the claim that any breach would result in 'serious and irreparable harm'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be surrendered or, at CMHC’s option, certified destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request. The Proponent shall notify CMHC immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that originals of any nature provided by CMHC shall be surrendered to CMHC immediately upon request. The Proponent shall notify CMHC upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic)' and 'Confidential Information' from the list of items to be returned, and removing 'immediately' after 'notify CMHC' the clause becomes significantly weaker. This directly contradicts the stated purpose of the agreement, which is to protect 'confidential and proprietary information' (Article 1). This is an in-text contradiction because the original intent to protect information is undermined by a weaker return of documents clause. Also by ommiting 'immediately' after 'notify CMHC' this means the Proponent doesn't have to surrender immediately any discovery, and creating an incertainty for the moment the discovery should be informed, undermining Article 3 because it's hard to know when a breach has been made. This creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" means all non-public information of CMHC and the Absence Management Services, and all personal information of CMHC employees, disclosed to the Proponent in aggregate or otherwise, including, without limitation, any functional, technical, operational and business information re lating to CMHC or its Absence Management Program including, but not limited to, demographics, policies, procedures, absence management details, financial data, product/service specifications and designs, data models, member data, medical data, return to work plans and communication plans. It also includes information orally disclosed if the disclosing party indicates at the time of disclosure the confidential or proprietary nature of the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the return of documents clause by omitting 'copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic)' and 'Confidential Information,' directly contradicting the agreement's purpose to protect 'confidential and proprietary information' as defined in Section 1.1. Section 1.1 defines Confidential Information, which the changed Section 2.3 no longer explicitly requires to be returned, creating a direct conflict. Also, by removing 'immediately' after 'notify CMHC,' the changed text introduces uncertainty about the timeframe for reporting unauthorized use or disclosure, which undermines the prompt action expected for managing confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The Proponent agrees to indemnify and hold harmless CMHC from and against all losses, expenses, claims and liability arising out of any breach by the Proponent or its affiliates, or their respective officers, employees or representatives of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 The Proponent agrees to indemnify and hold harmless CMHC from losses arising out of a direct breach by the Proponent of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'expenses, claims and liability' and 'its affiliates, or their respective officers, employees or representatives' from the indemnification clause, the scope of protection afforded to CMHC is severely limited. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 4.4 ('Entire Agreement') because the agreement no longer provides the comprehensive protection that the 'entire agreement' clause suggests it does. The changed text doesn't consider any breaches or claims made by the Proponent representatives, creating incertainty with Article 4.4 because this article states that all terms are applicable to the contract, but one of them has been cut down. Thus, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any other agreement or discussion, oral or written. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.3 provided comprehensive indemnification to CMHC, covering losses, expenses, claims, and liabilities arising from breaches by the Proponent, its affiliates, or their representatives. The changed text significantly narrows this scope by limiting it to only 'losses arising out of a direct breach by the Proponent'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4.4 ('Entire Agreement'), which states that the agreement constitutes the 'entire agreement' and is binding on all parties. The narrowed scope in the changed text means the agreement no longer provides the comprehensive protection that the 'entire agreement' clause suggests, as it excludes liabilities arising from affiliates or representatives of the Proponent. This omission creates an inconsistency and therefore, a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Notices. All notices required under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been given on the next day by fax or other electronic means or upon personal delivery, or in ten (10) days upon delivery in the mail, first class, with postage prepaid. Notices shall be sent to the addresses indicated below unless written notification of change of address shall have been given. If to CMHC: CANADA MORTGAGE AND HOUSING CORPORATION 700 Montreal Road Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0P7 Attention: [insert name] Fax: [insert fax no] If to Proponent: [insert details as above]
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Notices. All notices required under this Agreement shall be in writing. Notices shall be sent to the addresses indicated below. If to CMHC: CANADA MORTGAGE AND HOUSING CORPORATION 700 Montreal Road Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0P7 Attention: [insert name] If to Proponent: [insert details as above]
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the delivery times and methods for notices (fax, electronic means, mail), this creates an in-text contradiction because Article 4.8 'Continuing Obligations' makes it impossible to know when the obligations begun and when they end, because those obligations may or may not be enforced. This introduces uncertainty about when a notice is considered 'given', thus affecting enforcement of obligations under the agreement and undermining the 'Continuing Obligations' article. The omitted section includes delivery times and methods for notices, and is essential for enforcement of obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies delivery times and methods for notices, including fax, electronic means, and mail with specific timelines. The changed text omits these details, creating an in-text contradiction with Article 4.8 'Continuing Obligations'. Because the original text provides methods for giving notice which would trigger the obligations to begin or end, removing those elements impacts enforcement of the agreement, and undermines the 'Continuing Obligations' article, leading to a strong contradiction because of the uncertainty about when the obligations begin.

perturbation.original_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and confidentiality' and 'and the return of any Confidential Information' creates incertainty regarding if the proponent must keep confidential any information from CMHC after the termination of the contract. This undermines the main purpose of the NDA which is to protect the information, because according to the clause the Proponent can start disclosing the information when the contract has been terminated. This creates an in-text contradiction of the main porpuse of the NDA with a critical article of it. Also by removing the return of Confidential Information clause, it's a violation of Article 2 where is stated this information should be returned, which is creating an in-text contradiction by removing a vital aspect of protection of information for the NDA
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be surrendered or, at CMHC’s option, certified destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request. The Proponent shall notify CMHC immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'and confidentiality' and 'and the return of any Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because Article 2.3, the contradicted text, explicitly states that 'all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be surrendered or, at CMHC’s option, certified destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request'. By removing the 'return of any Confidential Information' clause in the changed text, it directly contradicts Article 2.3, leading to a clear and impactful in-text contradiction, because it means the proponent might not have the obligation to return all documents, which is against the NDA protection of information.
